---
title: "Approaches to biomedical knowledge"
subtitle: "Ontologies and Large Language Models"
author: "Peter N Robinson"
institute: "Free University Berlin"
date: 2026-04-26
jupyter: python3
format: 
  html:
    theme: [default] 
    css: style/styles.css
    logo: images/fu_berlin_logo.png 
    footer: "bioonto-llm"
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    incremental: false 
    html-math-method: mathjax
    margin: 0.1
    embed-resources: true
    transition: fade
    background-transition: convex
    controls: true
---



This course is held as a part of the [Master Curriculum](https://www.mi.fu-berlin.de/bioinf/stud/master/index.html) in Bioinformatics of the Free University Berlin.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This course enables students to master two essential frameworks for managing human knowledge in bioinformatics: **Ontologies** and **Language Models**
:::

### Lecture Schedule

*   [What to expect from this course](lectures/overview.qmd)
*   [Lecture 1: Ontologies: OBO, RDF, RDFS, and OWL](lectures/ontology1.qmd)
*   [Lecture 2: Gene Ontology (GO) and Overrepresentation Analysis](lectures/ontology2.qmd)
*   [Lecture 3: Bayesian algorithms for GO: Model-based gene-set analysis](lectures/ontology3.qmd)
*   [Lecture 4: Human Phenotype Ontology: Semantic similarity algorithms](lectures/ontology4.qmd)
*   [Lecture 5: From Logistic Regression to Neural Foundations](lectures/logreg.qmd)
*   [Lecture 6: Neural networks](lectures/neural.qmd)
*   Lecture 7: RNNs and LSTM (to do)
*   Lecture 8: Embeddings and Word2Vec (partially finisheed)
*   [Lecture 9: Encoders](lectures/encoder.qmd)
*   Lecture 10: Decoder architecture (to do)
*.  Lecture 11: BERT and Sentence BERT (to do)
*   Lecture 12: Reinforcement learning and LLMs (to do)
*   Lecture 13: RAG, Fine tuning, Structured Outputs  (to do)
*   Lecture 14: Applications of LLMs in Medicine: Problems and opportunities (Short lecture followed by review period)

### Homework

* Each lecture will be accompanied by a practical session to go over background material and review the homework.
